questions.questionText: A table has rows (4, 14), (8, 28), (12, 42). Is it proportional?
options: Yes
No
Only for the first two rows
Only if there is a graph
questions.explanation: The ratio y/x is 3.5 in every row.

questions.questionText: If the unit rate is 2.4, what is y when x = 25?
questions.explanation: Multiply 25 by 2.4.

questions.questionText: Which row belongs with the proportional table (3, 12), (5, 20)?
questions.explanation: The ratio is 4, so 7 should match 28.

questions.questionText: A table shows x = 2.5, 4, 10 and y = 5, 8, 20. What is the unit rate?
questions.explanation: Each y-value is 2 times x.

questions.questionText: Why is the table (6, 15), (14, 35), (20, 50) proportional?
options: Each output is 2.5 times the input
Each output is 9 more than the input
The inputs are even
The outputs end in 5 or 0
questions.correctAnswer: Each output is 2.5 times the input
questions.explanation: The ratio y/x is constant at 2.5.

questions.questionText: A row in a proportional table is (9, 31.5). What is y when x = 4?
questions.explanation: The ratio is 31.5/9 = 3.5, so 4 x 3.5 = 14.

questions.questionText: Why does the table (2, 7), (4, 14), (9, 31.5) fit together?
options: Each output is 3.5 times the input
Each output is 5 more than the input
Each input is doubled each time
The outputs all have decimals
questions.correctAnswer: Each output is 3.5 times the input
questions.explanation: The ratio y/x is 3.5 in every row.

questions.questionText: Which table is not proportional?
questions.explanation: If the ratio were 2.25, then 12 should match 27, not 26.

questions.questionText: A proportional table has rows (8, 6), (16, 12), (24, ?). What is the missing value?
questions.explanation: The ratio is 0.75, so 24 x 0.75 = 18.

questions.questionText: What is the best way to test whether a table is proportional?
options: Check whether the ratio is the same in every row
Check whether the difference is the same in every row
Check whether x increases each time
Check whether y is larger than x
questions.correctAnswer: Check whether the ratio is the same in every row
questions.explanation: Equal ratios prove proportionality.

questions.questionText: A table has x = 7, 14, 28 and y = 10.5, 21, 42. What is the unit rate?
questions.explanation: Divide y by x: 10.5/7 = 1.5.

questions.questionText: Which row belongs if the table has ratio 4.5 and x = 18?
questions.explanation: Multiply 18 by 4.5.

questions.questionText: A student wants to fill in a missing value in a proportional table. What should they use after finding the ratio?
options: The same multiplicative relationship on the missing row
A constant difference between rows
Only the first row of the table
A graph with random points
questions.correctAnswer: The same multiplicative relationship on the missing row
questions.explanation: The constant ratio must be applied consistently to the new row.

questions.questionText: A table has rows (10, 4.5), (20, 9), and (30, ?). What is the missing value?
questions.explanation: The ratio is 0.45, so 30 x 0.45 = 13.5.

questions.questionText: Which statement is true about a proportional table with row (12, 54)?
options: The unit rate could be 4.5
The unit rate must be 54
The table cannot be proportional
The input is larger than the output in every row
questions.explanation: Divide 54 by 12 to get the unit rate.

questions.questionText: A table has rows (2, 3.4), (5, 8.5), (11, ?). What is the missing value?
questions.explanation: The ratio is 1.7, so 11 x 1.7 = 18.7.

questions.questionText: Why is the table (3, 4.8), (10, 16), (15, 24) proportional?
options: Each output is 1.6 times the input
Each output is 1.6 more than the input
The differences match
The table includes decimals
questions.correctAnswer: Each output is 1.6 times the input
questions.explanation: The ratio y/x is 1.6 each time.

questions.questionText: If the unit rate is 11/4, what is y when x = 20?
questions.explanation: Multiply 20 by 11/4.

questions.questionText: Which row breaks the proportional table (4, 18), (8, 36), (15, 67.5), (20, 88)
questions.explanation: If the ratio were 4.5, then 20 should match 90, not 88.

questions.questionText: What is the best summary of how to use a proportional table?
options: Use the constant ratio to test, complete, and predict values
Use constant differences to build every row
Use only rows with whole numbers
Use a graph instead of the table
questions.correctAnswer: Use the constant ratio to test, complete, and predict values
questions.explanation: A proportional table is useful because one constant ratio controls all the rows.
